direction.description: RFC 1035 compression pointers require bounded, unambiguous, terminating name expansion.

spec_ref.section: RFC 1035 2.3.4 and 4.1.4; RFC 9267 RR processing anti-patterns
spec_ref.quote_or_summary: DNS labels and compressed names have length and pointer constraints; RFC 9267 documents common parser anti-patterns.

statement: A DNS compression pointer must resolve within the message, must not form cycles, and expanded names must preserve label and total-name length limits.
condition: A DNS parser decodes a compressed domain name from a peer-controlled packet.
expected_behavior: The parser validates pointer bounds, loop termination, label lengths, and expanded name length before using the name.
violation_pattern: The parser blindly follows pointers, allows cycles/out-of-bounds offsets, or expands names beyond RFC length limits.
bound_goal: DNS parser termination, unambiguous name decoding, and DoS/RCE prevention

review.review_notes: Use RFC 9267 as direction seed; later attach concrete CVE/advisory after selecting implementation.
